key_risks: Cooler-than-expected CPI/PPI sparks a relief rally in growth/tech, causing defensive tilt to lag SPY
Energy reverses if oil inventory data or geopolitics shift supply outlook
Healthcare drug-pricing policy headlines (CMS negotiation) pressure the sector
Defensive sectors underperform if equity risk appetite broadly recovers into FOMC

portfolio.rationale: Strong defensive momentum (+3.25% 7d, +6.38% 30d) amid recent tech selloff; lower beta heading into hot CPI/PPI prints that could pressure growth.
Crude at $91 WTI with EIA forecasting global inventory draws; energy positive recent momentum and benefits if inflation surprises hot.
Value held up best among broad styles (-0.08% 7d) and is less rate-sensitive into CPI/PPI/FOMC-positioning week.
Defensive sector positive (+1.27% 7d) with elevated inflation prints likely to favor staples over discretionary.
Cash-like ballast yielding positively with no duration risk into hot inflation data and FOMC-eve uncertainty.

portfolio_rationale: Defensive tilt into a week with hot CPI (June 10) and PPI (June 11) prints plus FOMC the day after exit; favor healthcare, energy, value, and staples that resisted the recent tech-led drawdown over rate-sensitive growth.
rationale_summary: Rotate toward defensive and inflation-resilient sectors ahead of CPI/PPI and FOMC positioning, avoiding the high-beta tech/growth that led the recent selloff.
